query: I regressed outcome Y on treatment T and got a positive slope of +0.5. Then I added a control variable C to the regression and the slope flipped to -0.3. Which estimate is the correct causal effect of T on Y?
expected_behavior: Refuses to declare one of the two estimates correct without first resolving the structural role of C (confounder, collider, or mediator) in the underlying causal DAG, and explains that the same data pattern is consistent with multiple DAGs that imply different correct estimates

Names at least three distinct structural scenarios for C — for example confounder (then adjusted is correct), collider (then unadjusted is correct), and mediator (then both are correct but estimate different estimands such as total vs direct effect) — and connects each to a different sign-flip outcome

Warns against reverse-engineering the DAG to match whichever sign feels more plausible, and instead requires committing to the DAG (temporal order, hypothesized edges) BEFORE letting the regression results pick the adjustment
